title: Better user experience through better (less!) logging
body:
## In what area(s)?

<!-- Remove the '> ' to select -->

/area runtime

## Describe the feature

As someone who develops with and supports dapr, I've started thinking about how the overall experience with the dapr sidecar can be improved. Getting to the bottom of a dapr issue - even the most minor - often requires scanning through dozens of lines of logs, looking for that one needle in a haystack of nominal output. 

The default logging at the level we see today is handy for an internals developer who wants to see each lifecycle checkpoint being hit. But as someone who is hoping to have a low-touch experience, it's not of much interest, and can even be overwhelming.

Let's take the actors quickstart server as an example.  This log output is from me running it, but with one mistake (it's up to you to spot it 😈 ):

```
ℹ️  Starting Dapr with id actorservice. HTTP Port: 45565. gRPC Port: 41647
ℹ️  Checking if Dapr sidecar is listening on HTTP port 45565
INFO[0000] Starting Dapr Runtime -- version 1.15.11 -- commit 76034a63a329003e6f56f1da7cbd9a2481469592  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Log level set to: info                        app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
WARN[0000] mTLS is disabled. Skipping certificate request and tls validation  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.security type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Enabled features: SchedulerReminders          app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] metric spec: {"enabled":true}                 app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.diagnostics type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Using default latency distribution buckets: [1 2 3 4 5 6 8 10 13 16 20 25 30 40 50 65 80 100 130 160 200 250 300 400 500 650 800 1000 2000 5000 10000 20000 50000 100000]  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.diagnostics type=log ver=1.15.11
WARN[0000] The default value for 'spec.metric.http.increasedCardinality' will change to 'false' in Dapr 1.15 or later  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.diagnostics type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] standalone mode configured                    app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] app id: actorservice                          app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Dapr trace sampler initialized: ParentBased{root:AlwaysOnSampler,remoteParentSampled:AlwaysOnSampler,remoteParentNotSampled:AlwaysOffSampler,localParentSampled:AlwaysOnSampler,localParentNotSampled:AlwaysOffSampler}  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] metrics server started on 0.0.0.0:43811/      app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] local service entry announced: actorservice -> 192.168.1.234:38505  app_id=actorservice component="nr (mdns/v1)" instance=alcyra scope=dapr.contrib type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Initialized name resolution to mdns           app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Loading components…                           app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Waiting for all outstanding components to be processed…  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Connected and received scheduler hosts addresses: [localhost:50006]  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.scheduler.watchhosts type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Scheduler client initialized for address: localhost:50006  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.scheduler.clients type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Scheduler clients initialized                 app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.scheduler.clients type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Using 'statestore' as actor state store       app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.processor.state type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Component loaded: statestore (state.redis/v1)  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.processor type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] All outstanding components processed          app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Loading endpoints…                            app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Waiting for all outstanding http endpoints to be processed…  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] All outstanding http endpoints processed      app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Loading Declarative Subscriptions…            app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
WARN[0000] App channel is not initialized. Did you configure an app-port?  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.channels type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] gRPC server listening on TCP address: :41647  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.grpc.api type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Enabled gRPC tracing middleware               app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.grpc.api type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Enabled gRPC metrics middleware               app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.grpc.api type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Registering workflow engine for gRPC endpoint: [::]:41647  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.grpc.api type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] API gRPC server is running on port 41647      app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
WARN[0000] The default value for 'spec.metric.http.increasedCardinality' will change to 'false' in Dapr 1.15 or later  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.http type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Enabled max body size HTTP middleware with size 4194304 bytes  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.http type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Enabled tracing HTTP middleware               app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.http type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Enabled metrics HTTP middleware               app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.http type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] HTTP server listening on TCP address: :45565  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.http type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] HTTP server is running on port 45565          app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] The request body size parameter is: 4194304 bytes  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] gRPC server listening on TCP address: :38505  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.grpc.internal type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Enabled gRPC tracing middleware               app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.grpc.internal type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Enabled gRPC metrics middleware               app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.grpc.internal type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Internal gRPC server is running on :38505     app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Using Scheduler service for reminders.        app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actor.reminders.scheduler type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Registering hosted actors: []                 app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actor type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] dapr initialized. Status: Running. Init Elapsed 15ms  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Actor runtime started                         app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actor type=log ver=1.15.11
WARN[0000] Graceful shutdown timeout is infinite, will wait indefinitely to shutdown  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actor type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Reporting actor types: []                     app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actors.placement type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Scheduler stream connected for [JOB_TARGET_TYPE_JOB]  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.scheduler.cluster type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Connected to placement localhost:50005        app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actors.placement.client.connector.static type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] worker started with backend dapr.actors/v1    app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.wfengine.durabletask.backend type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Workflow engine started                       app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.wfengine type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Connected to placement localhost:50005        app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actors.placement.client type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Placement tables updated, version: 0          app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actors.placement type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Running actor reminder migration from state store to scheduler  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actors.reminders.migration type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Skipping migration, no missing scheduler reminders found  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actors.reminders.migration type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Found 0 missing scheduler reminders from state store  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actors.reminders.migration type=log ver=1.15.11
INFO[0000] Migrated 0 reminders from state store to scheduler successfully  app_id=actorservice instance=alcyra scope=dapr.runtime.actors.reminders.migration type=log ver=1.15.11
ℹ️  Checking if Dapr sidecar is listening on GRPC port 41647
ℹ️  Dapr sidecar is up and running.
ℹ️  Updating metadata for appPID: 404325
ℹ️  Updating metadata for app command: dotnet run --urls=http://localhost:5001/
✅  You're up and running! Both Dapr and your app logs will appear here.

== APP == info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[14]
== APP ==       Now listening on: http://localhost:5001
== APP == info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
== APP ==       Application started. Press Ctrl+C to shut down.
== APP == info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
== APP ==       Hosting environment: Production
== APP == info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
== APP ==       Content root path: /home/atrauzzi/Development/atrauzzi/dapr-quickstarts/actors/csharp/sdk/service
```

Using the above logging output as a non-exhaustive example, here's what I suggest:

### Drop

| Log Line (truncated) | Suggested Action | Rationale |
|-----------------------|------------------|-----------|
| `ℹ️ Checking if Dapr sidecar is listening on HTTP port 45565` | Drop | Nominal state, not actionable. |
| `INFO Starting Dapr Runtime -- version ...` | Merge | Fold into first log output. |
| `INFO Log level set to: info` | Merge | Can be part of first log line. |
| `INFO Enabled features: SchedulerReminders` | Drop | Defaults, not user-controlled. |
| `INFO metric spec: {"enabled":true}` | Debug | Internal diagnostics, not user-facing. |
| `INFO Using default latency distribution buckets: [...]` | Debug | Internal detail. |
| `WARN The default value for 'spec.metric.http.increasedCardinality' will change...` | Drop | User didn’t configure this; unnecessary warning. 
| `INFO standalone mode configured` | Merge | Belongs in first log line. |
| `INFO app id: actorservice` | Drop | Already reported multiple times. |
| `INFO Dapr trace sampler initialized: ParentBased...` | Drop | Repeats defaults. |
| `INFO metrics server started on 0.0.0.0:43811/` | Merge | Include in first log line. |
| `INFO local service entry announced: actorservice -> ...` | Trace | Low-level service discovery. |
| `INFO Initialized name resolution to mdns` | Debug | Default, not useful to most users. |
| `INFO Loading components…` | Drop | Component success/failure is enough. |
| `INFO Waiting for all outstanding components...` | Drop | Internal lifecycle, not actionable. |
| `INFO Connected and received scheduler hosts addresses...` | Trace | Debug-only detail. |
| `INFO Scheduler client initialized for address: ...` | Drop | Only matters on failure. |
| `INFO Scheduler clients initialized` | Drop | Redundant. |
| `INFO Using 'statestore' as actor state store` | Drop | Failure would be evident during use. |
| `INFO All outstanding components processed` | Drop | Internal. |
| `INFO Loading endpoints…` | Drop | Endpoints themselves matter, not the loading step. |
| `INFO Waiting for all outstanding http endpoints...` | Drop | Internal. |
| `INFO All outstanding http endpoints processed` | Drop | Internal. |
| `INFO Loading Declarative Subscriptions…` | Drop | Only discovered subs are relevant. |
| `INFO gRPC server listening on TCP address: :41647` | Merge | Consolidate into first log line. |
| `INFO Enabled gRPC tracing middleware` | Drop | Default noise. |
| `INFO Enabled gRPC metrics middleware` | Drop | Default noise. |
| `INFO Registering workflow engine for gRPC endpoint: ...` | Debug | Only relevant to debugging. |
| `INFO API gRPC server is running on port 41647` | Drop | Redundant. |
| `WARN The default value for 'spec.metric.http.increasedCardinality' will change...` | Drop | Repeated, not user-configured. |
| `INFO Enabled max body size HTTP middleware...` | Drop | Defaults, not critical. |
| `INFO Enabled tracing HTTP middleware` | Drop | Defaults, not critical. |
| `INFO Enabled metrics HTTP middleware` | Drop | Defaults, not critical. |
| `INFO HTTP server listening on TCP address: :45565` | Merge | Fold into first log output. |
| `INFO HTTP server is running on port 45565` | Drop | Redundant. |
| `INFO The request body size parameter is: 4194304 bytes` | Debug | Internal default. |
| `INFO gRPC server listening on TCP address: :38505` | Merge | Fold into first log output. |
| `INFO Enabled gRPC tracing middleware (internal)` | Drop | Default noise. |
| `INFO Enabled gRPC metrics middleware (internal)` | Drop | Default noise. |
| `INFO Internal gRPC server is running on :38505` | Merge | Fold into first log output. |
| `INFO Using Scheduler service for reminders.` | Trace | Only relevant during transition. |
| `INFO Registering hosted actors: []` | Drop | Not clearly defined, empty output. |
| `INFO Actor runtime started` | Drop | Redundant. |
| `WARN Graceful shutdown timeout is infinite...` | Drop | Low value, adds noise. |
| `INFO Reporting actor types: []` | Drop | Empty, no useful info. |
| `INFO Scheduler stream connected for [JOB_TARGET_TYPE_JOB]` | Debug | Internal. |
| `INFO Connected to placement localhost:50005` | Trace | Only useful for troubleshooting. |
| `INFO worker started with backend dapr.actors/v1` | Trace | Debugging detail. |
| `INFO Workflow engine started` | Drop | Redundant. |
| `INFO Connected to placement localhost:50005 (again)` | Trace | Duplicate. |
| `INFO Placement tables updated, version: 0` | Debug | Internal. |
| `INFO Running actor reminder migration from state store...` | Consolidate | Only output if action taken. |
| `INFO Skipping migration, no missing scheduler reminders...` | Consolidate | Same as above. |
| `INFO Found 0 missing scheduler reminders...` | Consolidate | Same as above. |
| `INFO Migrated 0 reminders from state store...` | Consolidate | Same as above. |
| `ℹ️ Checking if Dapr sidecar is listening on GRPC port 41647` | Drop | Nominal state. |
| `ℹ️ Updating metadata for appPID: ...` | Drop | Internal. |
| `ℹ️ Updating metadata for app command: dotnet run...` | Drop | Internal. |

### Keep

| Log Line (truncated) | Suggested Action | Rationale |
|-----------------------|------------------|-----------|
| `ℹ️ Starting Dapr with id actorservice. HTTP Port: 45565. gRPC Port: 41647, Internal gRPC Port: 38505, Metrics Port: 43811` | Keep | Single consolidated startup line with app id + ports. |
| `INFO Dapr Runtime v1.15.11 (commit ...) in standalone mode, log level=info` | Keep | Version, mode, commit, and log level are key context. |
| `WARN mTLS is disabled. Skipping certificate request and TLS validation` | Keep | Security-relevant visibility. |
| `INFO Component loaded: statestore (state.redis/v1)` | Keep | Confirms critical state store component loaded. |
| `WARN App channel is not initialized. Did you configure an app-port?` | Keep | Actionable warning if app-port is missing. |
| `INFO dapr initialized. Status: Running. Init Elapsed 15ms` | Keep | Confirms runtime ready with elapsed time. |
| `ℹ️ Dapr sidecar is up and running (elapsed: 0.32s)` | Keep | User-facing confirmation of readiness. |
| `✅ You're up and running! Both Dapr and your app logs will appear here.` | Keep | Friendly end-state marker, signals successful startup. |


### The End Result?

Here's an imagining of what this output could have looked like:

```
ℹ️  Starting Dapr with id actorservice. HTTP Port: 45565. gRPC Port: 41647, Internal gRPC Port: 38505, Metrics Port: 43811
INFO[0000] Dapr Runtime v1.15.11 (commit 76034a63a3) in standalone mode, log level=info
WARN[0000] mTLS is disabled. Skipping certificate request and TLS validation
INFO[0000] Component loaded: statestore (state.redis/v1)
WARN[0000] App channel is not initialized. Did you configure an app-port?
INFO[0000] dapr initialized. Status: Running. Init Elapsed 15ms
ℹ️  Dapr sidecar is up and running (elapsed: 0.32s)
✅  You're up and running! Both Dapr and your app logs will appear here.

== APP == info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[14]
== APP ==       Now listening on: http://localhost:5001
== APP == info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
== APP ==       Application started. Press Ctrl+C to shut down.
== APP == info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
== APP ==       Hosting environment: Production
== APP == info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
== APP ==       Content root path: /home/atrauzzi/Development/atrauzzi/dapr-quickstarts/actors/csharp/sdk/service
```

Now, the user experience has improved dramatically and any real configuration issues are much easier to spot at a glance.

---

## Rationale

I can appreciate that a dapr developer or seasoned user might have a very workable heuristic for spotting the issue above.  But I think we need to put that to one side.  Most dapr newcomers and people dipping their toes into dapr deployments will struggle with the original output above.  It becomes a game of _you don't know what you don't know_, and here's why:

 - At no point does dapr report an error
 - The dotnet application by its best account comes online and appears healthy
 - Dapr even comes with a reassuring green checkmark
 - Log output that might warrant action is surrounded by nominal output that 99.9~% of the time requires no user action
 - The sheer volume of log output is overwhelming, especially when in a live environment and is filling up our users logging systems ($$$$!)
 - People naturally self-condition to block out information when it isn't useful or too dense

I think for now, a discussion about dapr failure states can be saved for later.  But we can definitely see from the above that we could give users a much better shot at identifying their issues and possibly even resolving them.  At least before having to engage other resources for help -- or worse -- getting frustrated and giving up.

For this, let's be ambitious and adopt the rationale where info and warn logging is only done if necessary and only if an inevitable failure wouldn't otherwise highlight an issue.

---

## Release Note
<!-- How should this new feature be announced in our release notes? It can be populated later. -->
<!-- Keep it as a single line. Examples: -->

<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **ADD** New feature in Dapr. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Bug in runtime. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **UPDATE** Runtime dependency. -->

RELEASE NOTE:

comments.body: This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has not had activity in the last 90 days. It will be closed in the next 7 days unless it is tagged (pinned, good first issue, help wanted or triaged/resolved) or other activity occurs. Thank you for your contributions.

This issue has been automatically closed because it has not had activity in the last 97 days. If this issue is still valid, please ping a maintainer and ask them to label it as pinned, good first issue, help wanted or triaged/resolved. Thank you for your contributions.
